frameworks.name: adaptive (non-linear) evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: evidence integration that adjusts to hidden environmental change points

frameworks.name: recurrent cortical microcircuit
frameworks.one_line: circuit-level mechanism proposed to implement the adaptive accumulation

frameworks.name: pupil-linked arousal modulation
frameworks.one_line: arousal as a gain signal shaping sensory-cortex state during accumulation

core_question: How does the human cortex implement non-linear, adaptive evidence accumulation in environments with hidden state changes?
key_assumptions: optimal accuracy in changing environments requires non-linear accumulation
MEG population activity indexes decision-related cortical dynamics
action-encoding parietal/frontal regions carry accumulation signals
manipulations: visual evidence accumulation task with hidden state changes
key_findings: behavior shows hallmarks of adaptive (non-linear) evidence accumulation
action-planning parietal/frontal regions exhibit matching decision dynamics
frequency-specific modulation of visual cortex mirrors decision state
this sensory modulation depends on pupil-linked arousal and change probability
interpretations: adaptive accumulation can be realized by a recurrent cortical microcircuit
decision-related feedback to sensory cortex is itself adaptive to environmental statistics
